## [00:00 - 03:07]

This is the pubcast shot. I'm John Loomer Your performance goal is everything Let's go So on a prior episode I talked about how the algorithm works and this is critical to understand So go back and listen if you haven't the short version though is that the algorithm Is literal about how it works so you define what you want in the algorithm tries to get you that action So today what we're going to do is dig into how you can go about this It's the performance goal that defines what you want So this is defined in the ad set it controls ad delivery and optimization options for your performance goal include things like Maximize number of conversions maximize value of conversions Maximize link clicks maximize landing page views Maximize through play views and on and on and on there were 21 different performance goals last I counted But as far as I know there could be more now Since this is how you define what you want and how your ads are delivered you can't overstate Its importance so targeting is mostly algorithmic now if advantage plus audience is on virtually everything is a suggestion and Even if you turn advantage plus audience off Detailed targeting and look like audiences are often expanded which basically means that they are Suggestions as well and how does algorithmic targeting work? well Meta will show your ads to the people most likely to perform the action that you want and how do we define that? With your performance goal so in other words your performance goal acts as your targeting now If it doesn't completely determine your targeting it does play a huge part I get it that these days you might feel like you have absolutely no control over targeting But you do so you got to understand this you're going to reach different people Depending on your performance goal so the targeting changes even if you don't know what the details of that targeting are this is also how your performance goal can lead to high quality or Low quality people depending on your choice. So let's talk this through you've defined your performance goal as Maximize link clicks the algorithm says cool Let me find you as many link clicks as possible to make you happy now The algorithm might show your ad to a few people who have been known to click on your stuff before It could sprinkle in a few people who visited your website or bought from you the intention isn't to find perspective customers though So it really doesn't need to show it to those people going after your remarketing audience may actually be more expensive

## [03:07 - 06:10]

Then going after a different group. Why would that be? Well, there's an audience just waiting for you on the audience network placement in that case It doesn't matter what your business is or what's in your ad the audience network placement Generates a ton of cheap clicks now sure most of them are accidental clicks or Even click fraud before it's detected, but you know what that? Technically doesn't matter you wanted cheap clicks and that's where they're gonna be found now Even if you say okay, I'll remove audience network That's not gonna completely solve the problem The algorithm is still only focused on finding cheap clicks and it will find them because some people simply click on everything I don't know why but they do some people are confused Some people may not be people at all. Some people are bots spam Whatever it's funny how when it comes to engagement and clicks and these kinds of things you always find Cheap versions low quality versions. That's never a problem when it comes to a purchase, right? So now let's change the performance goal to maximize number of conversions Now once you do that you also need to select a conversion event. So yeah, let's select purchase this time So at that point everything changes the algorithm still wants to get you as many of that action as it can It aims to please but the weaknesses that it could exploit before are different now It can't simply find cheap or accidental purchases from a placement because that's not a thing So first of all your ads probably won't even be shown on that audience network placement It's crazy how that happens But second your audience is going to completely change without doing anything to your targeting So now you can expect a sizable percentage of your budget to be spent on remarketing so prior customers your email list Website visitors people have engaged with your ads before. Why is that? It's because meta knows that these people are some of those who are most likely to buy from you So you've just changed the targeting by changing the performance goal Now even when you change the conversion event the targeting will change not explicitly once again But ultimately who sees your ads so while the performance goal is maximize conversions Change the conversion event to leads the audience will change again Now while you will likely see far more remarketing in this case Then you would for if you were optimized for link clicks It will probably be quite a bit less than when you wanted purchases because unlike purchases Leads can have weaknesses that are exploitable to get you cheap actions

## [06:10 - 09:07]

And while you'd love to get cheap sales, you don't necessarily want the cheapest of leads now You want the cheapest high quality leads, but the algorithm doesn't necessarily know that so this is where I found the algorithm exploit weaknesses by age as an example now for whatever reason people over the age of 65 Seem to complete a whole lot of subscription forms So unless I set an age maximum when optimizing for leads my budget will suddenly move to this age group Very high percentage of it. So the bottom line is this your performance goal is really important So do not get cute by trying to get cheap clicks or video views or something else Thinking that this is the way to trick the algorithm to getting you cheap sales somehow And I'd even say the same thing about going add to cart or initiate checkout or view content Could be the same thing. It's extremely unlikely that this approach is gonna work now While you're losing control over explicit targeting these days, this is still one of the main ways You can control who sees your ads. So do not minimize it Do not complain about low quality results when you use a performance goal It doesn't consider quality. There's also a whole other topic there about how I wish Meta would find a way to allow us to optimize for quality actions for top of the funnel But that's another discussion when possible use maximize conversions and the purchase conversion event When you use anything else know the risks involved and do what you can to make a thumb Now I wrote a long blog post about inflated results and what you can do to address them That provides a whole roadmap for addressing them based on the various performance goals Go check it out at John Lumer comm slash inflated One last thing so if you're a meta advertiser and you need a little extra support I have something for you.
